title: clerodendrum triplinerve var. sulcatum
intro: Clerodendrum triplinerve var. sulcatum, also known as the Sulcate Glory Bower or Sulcate Clerodendrum, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its distinctive, sulcate (grooved) leaves and fragrant, white flowers. The medicinal properties of Clerodendrum triplinerve var. sulcatum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Clerodendrum triplinerve var. sulcatum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat ailments such as coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
